text: Summary of Andrew and Jesse's Live Space combined with some feedback from his conversation with IG this week. * Note all interpretations are my own. The higher quality treasury companies continue to strengthen operationally despite recent share price sell-offs. Compression of global mNAVs attributed to Bitcoin’s weaker performance relative to prior years. Gold remains a leading indicator for longer-term Bitcoin performance. Andrew acknowledged the share price challenge but emphasised it’s only one metric - all other performance indicators are improving. Reiterated that while they can’t control the market, they remain firmly on track to grow Bitcoin per share over the longer term. SWC currently trades at a ~10% discount to BTC per share yet continues to generate Bitcoin yield, even if small. Currently using the most conservative metric using fully diluted shares, reflecting 0.9x mNAV. Scale (balance sheet size) remains a key focus for future strategic goals. Balance sheet remains extremely strong - £200m with no debt. Would not look to issue equity below 1.1x mNAV. Significant work being done on the SWC convertible bond. Standard debt remains an option at responsible levels and attractive rates. On preferred shares, they’d welcome Strategy’s entrance into the UK and Europe to build market education and prove out the model. Competition with Strategy would not be negative - institutions value multiple providers. Reminder: preferreds require a strong balance sheet to issue. Would expect SWC's balance sheet to be at least a "low multiple" higher than current levels as a potential starting point. Strive raised $160m this week (~6,000 BTC), yielding 12% (2% above Strategy). Noted the market doesn’t need to become a “race to the bottom” ; ~8% yield would still be attractive in the UK. The UK Budget could enable pension funds to allocate more to UK equities. Thanked the community for their ongoing support.

engagement.note: These figures were correct as of May 14, 2026, 9:00 AM London time
